davenport.neighborhoods: Downtown, Village of East Davenport, Prospect Hill
davenport.meetingHighlights: Part of the Quad Cities with a mix of downtown and neighborhood meetings; several evening and speaker meetings.
davenport.transitInfo: Davenport is served by Bettendorf Transit and regional bus connections; easy access via I-74 and I-80/280 nearby.

des-moines.neighborhoods: Downtown, East Village, Sherman Hill, Beaverdale, Drake, West Des Moines (nearby)
des-moines.meetingHighlights: Large and diverse meeting schedule across downtown and suburbs, with many lunchtime, evening, and specialty groups.
des-moines.transitInfo: Des Moines Area Regional Transit (DART) provides city bus service; Des Moines International Airport serves national flights.

cedar-rapids.neighborhoods: Downtown, NewBo, Czech Village, Kingston, Bever Park
cedar-rapids.meetingHighlights: Strong community meetings with several specialty and open discussion groups, plus active newcomer support.
cedar-rapids.transitInfo: CR Transit provides local buses; regional driving via I-380 and US-151.

iowa-city.meetingHighlights: University-influenced meeting schedule with many student-friendly, lunchtime, and evening groups; strong service structure.
iowa-city.transitInfo: Iowa City Transit serves local routes; close to Eastern Iowa Airport for regional flights.

sioux-city.meetingHighlights: Regional meetings serving northwest Iowa with steady attendance and variety of meeting formats.
sioux-city.transitInfo: Sioux City Transit provides local routes; Sioux Gateway Airport serves regional flights.

ames.meetingHighlights: College-town meetings with schedules catering to students and local residents; active noon and evening meetings.
ames.transitInfo: CyRide and regional buses provide local transit; easy driving access via I-35.

waterloo.meetingHighlights: Neighborhood and downtown meetings with a mix of formats; shared resources with Cedar Falls meetings.
waterloo.transitInfo: MET Transit provides local bus service; regional highway access via US-20.

dubuque.areaDescription: Dubuque and the tri-state area along the Mississippi River
dubuque.neighborhoods: Downtown Dubuque, Port of Dubuque, Cathedral District, West End, Asbury, Key West
dubuque.meetingHighlights: Mississippi River bluff-town recovery community with meetings in the Cathedral District and downtown; tri-state fellowship drawing members from Iowa, Illinois, and Wisconsin; strong tradition of speaker and step-study meetings.
dubuque.transitInfo: Jule Transit provides local bus service; Dubuque Regional Airport (DBQ); US-20 and US-61/151 provide regional highway access.

council-bluffs.areaDescription: Council Bluffs and Pottawattamie County across the Missouri River from Omaha
council-bluffs.neighborhoods: Downtown Council Bluffs, Broadway corridor, Lake Manawa area, Westside, North Broadway
council-bluffs.meetingHighlights: Metro-area recovery community with cross-river fellowship shared with Omaha, Nebraska; evening and weekend groups in downtown churches and community centers; strong newcomer welcome.
council-bluffs.transitInfo: Southwest Iowa Transit Agency (SWITA) and Metro Area Transit (MAT) buses connect to Omaha; Eppley Airfield (OMA) in Omaha; I-80 and I-29 interchange.

west-des-moines.areaDescription: Major western suburb in the Des Moines metropolitan area
west-des-moines.neighborhoods: Historic Valley Junction, Jordan Creek, Glen Oaks, West Glen, Southwoods, Grand Avenue corridor
west-des-moines.meetingHighlights: Meetings serve the west side of the Des Moines metro with convenient suburban access and steady attendance.
west-des-moines.transitInfo: DART buses connect West Des Moines with the wider metro; I-35, I-80, and I-235 serve the city.

ankeny.areaDescription: North Des Moines suburb in Polk County
ankeny.neighborhoods: Uptown Ankeny, Prairie Trail, Delaware corridor, Otter Creek area, Oralabor Road district, Georgetown
ankeny.meetingHighlights: Meetings serve a fast-growing suburban population north of Des Moines with regular local options.
ankeny.transitInfo: Road access via I-35 and Ankeny Boulevard; DART regional service connects select destinations.

mason-city.areaDescription: North Iowa regional hub in Cerro Gordo County
mason-city.neighborhoods: Downtown, River City district, East Park area, West Side, North End, South Monroe corridor
mason-city.meetingHighlights: Meetings serve north Iowa communities with dependable weekly schedules in a regional service center.
mason-city.transitInfo: Mason City Transit provides local routes; US-18, I-35 nearby, and Mason City Municipal Airport support travel.
